title: OpenJoystickDriver Device Catalog
description: Maps USB VID/PID pairs to parser implementations. Add new controllers here to get automatic driver support. All numeric values must be decimal — never hex.

version.description: Catalog format version (semver). Bump minor when adding devices, major when changing structure.

devices.description: Registered controllers. Each entry maps one USB VID+PID to a parser. Order does not matter — lookup is by VID:PID key.

vendor_id.description: USB Vendor ID in DECIMAL. Example: 13623 is 0x3537 (Gamesir). Look up VID/PID with 'system_profiler SPUSBDataType' on macOS.

product_id.description: USB Product ID in DECIMAL. Example: 4112 is 0x1010 (Gamesir G7 SE).

name.description: Full human-readable product name shown in UI and logs. Example: 'Gamesir G7 SE'.

short_name.description: Compact alphanumeric ID used in filenames, log prefixes, and profile keys. No spaces or symbols. Example: 'G7SE'.

parser.description: Parser class to use. GIP = Xbox Gaming Input Protocol (USB class 0xFF). DS4 = Sony DualShock 4 HID. GenericHID = best-effort fallback for unrecognized HID gamepads.

usb_class.description: USB bDeviceClass value. 255 (0xFF) = vendor-specific, handled via LibUSB/SwiftUSB. 3 (0x03) = HID class, handled via IOKit.
